category_title: Young children with anxiety exhibiting internalizing 'Freeze' or 'Flight' behaviors
minimal_context: The student is presented with a magnetic set and a picture to build a vehicle during an initial play session.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Here is the magnetic set and a picture of a vehicle. We are going to use the magnets to build this vehicle.

STUDENT: (Stays back and watches instead of joining in, remaining quiet and withdrawn.)

TUTOR: You can start putting the pieces together now.

STUDENT: (Refuses to complete the work.) I don't feel well.

TUTOR: Just try to build a little bit of it.

STUDENT: (Goes into a shell on his own, not making eye contact with anyone. His legs are shaking.) I am freezing. I am cold.

options.A: Pat the student on the shoulder, smile, and ask him to try building another part of the vehicle.
options.B: Prioritize the student's production of any action that could be taken as a response over their compliance with the tutor's suggestion.
options.C: Use a visual chart for checking in where specific numbers, colours, and faces related to anxiety are matched with feeling words.
options.D: Have more new motivating items handy to present to the student during the current magnetic building activity.
